profile.name: Joseph
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to lead financial discussions and keep the paperwork organized.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (comfortable with account types, taxes, and trade-offs; likes to reconcile figures and ask for precise definitions).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but disciplined: willing to take market risk if the plan is structured, diversified, and tied to income needs; gets uneasy with vague strategies or concentrated bets and will push for downside scenarios.
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-oriented; will reference specific dollar amounts and correct small inaccuracies. Uses occasional hedges like "if I’m remembering right" but generally speaks with confidence; asks clarifying questions about tax impact and account ownership.
profile.decision_making_style: Research-and-verify: prefers a clear recommendation, then wants to see assumptions, fees, and tax consequences before agreeing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; open to guidance but expects competence, transparency, and follow-through.
internal_biases: loss aversion (focuses on avoiding large drawdowns)
status quo bias (hesitates to change long-held accounts unless the benefit is clear)
hidden_concerns: outliving assets and maintaining stable cash flow without taking on debt
whether current insurance costs are still justified versus other priorities

profile.name: Patricia
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on lifestyle, monthly spending comfort, and peace of mind, while relying on Joseph for most account-level details.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (understands budgeting and the idea of retirement accounts vs. regular accounts, but mixes up terms like gross vs. net or premium vs. benefit occasionally).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative in day-to-day feelings: prefers predictability and cash buffers; can accept moderate risk if reassured that monthly expenses are covered and there’s a clear safety net.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; speaks in round numbers and approximations ("around," "give or take"). Will pause to check with Joseph on specifics; asks practical questions about what changes would mean month-to-month.
emotional_tendencies: seeks reassurance when markets or insurance topics come up
relief when there is a simple, repeatable plan
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven: wants to feel heard and comfortable, then supports Joseph’s final call once the plan is explained in plain language.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting if the advisor is patient and explains without jargon; trust drops if she feels rushed or talked over.
internal_biases: availability bias (recent market headlines can sway her comfort level)
mental accounting (separates "cash" as untouchable even if the plan suggests otherwise)
hidden_concerns: maintaining independence and avoiding financial complexity as they age
fear of unexpected large expenses and not wanting to be a burden
